summary: Applied the first low-risk ADR 0065 live rotation for `windmill_database_password`, repaired the live play path for separate worktrees and OpenBao metadata writes, and restored Windmill on docker-runtime-lv3 with verified OpenBao metadata and controller-local secret mirroring.

verification.observed: `python3 scripts/secret_rotation.py --validate` and `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_secret_rotation.py'` both passed on the integrated checkout used for the live apply.
`make syntax-check-secret-rotation` passed after fixing the runtime var loading, recursion, and OpenBao metadata-key rendering defects uncovered during the first live run.

A final explicit-value `windmill_database_password` apply completed successfully, including PostgreSQL password change on `postgres-lv3`, Windmill runtime restart on `docker-runtime-lv3`, controller-local password mirroring, OpenBao KV write, and OpenBao metadata update.
`curl -sS -o /tmp/windmill-version.out -w '%{http_code}' http://100.118.189.95:8005/api/version` returned `200`, and the API reported `CE v1.662.0` after the final reconciliation run.

The dedicated path `services/windmill/database-password` reported `current_version: 2` with `lv3_last_rotated` set to `2026-03-23T06:59:36Z` and `lv3_last_rotated_by` set to `repo-managed-secret-rotation`.

notes: The live apply surfaced and fixed three repo issues before the successful final run: recursive role vars in `playbooks/secret-rotation.yml`, missing runtime access to generated platform facts in the separate-worktree rotation path, and literal Jinja metadata keys in the OpenBao metadata body.

The guest firewall on `docker-runtime-lv3` also needed a repo-managed forward-path allowance for Docker bridge egress so container workloads can reach approved internal services such as `postgres-lv3:5432`.
